message:
🌱 Add flowise: [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to Connected mission (#984)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "flowise-4570-bug-composio-auth-status-fails-to-update-to-connected",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "flowise: [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to Connected",
+    "description": "[BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to Connected. This issue affects 5+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify flowise troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your flowise deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review flowise configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant flowise configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise\nkubectl get configmap -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flowise\n```\nWhen adding a Tool node to Agentflow 3.0.1 using the Composio tool, authentication succeeds and integration data is retrieved, but the integration status does not update to \"Active\". As a result, the node fails to execute properly."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to Connected",
+        "description": "- Added support for no auth apps in composio.\n- Added option to select connected account as user can have multiple connected accounts for the same app.\n- Fixes #4570 \n\n Note:\n- The currently used package composio-core is deprecated.\n- Spent a couple days in dependency-hell trying to upgrade to the newer /core package but that requires a lot of unrelated changes due to zod version being upgraded to 4.x which introduces major breaking changes in other unrelated areas as it is used as a peer\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade flowise to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade flowise:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --namespace flowise\n```\nVerify the upgrade:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n flowise\nhelm list -n flowise\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n flowise --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "- Added support for no auth apps in composio.\n- Added option to select connected account as user can have multiple connected accounts for the same app.\n- Fixes #4570 \n\n Note:\n- The currently used package composio-core is deprecated.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "flowise",
+      "community",
+      "ai-app",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "flowise"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/FlowiseAI/Flowise/issues/4570",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/FlowiseAI/Flowise",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/FlowiseAI/Flowise/pull/5614"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 16,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with flowise installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
